text:
Rodolfo Cossovich
Rodolfo Cossovich is an Assistant Arts Professor of Interactive Media Arts (IMA) at NYU Shanghai. His wide range of expertise in open-source robotics, educational technology, electric bikes, and assistive technologies, has attracted investment from MiLa Capital, Microsoft, and Kickstarter. His projects have also been featured in major press outlets such as Popular Mechanics, CNN, and CCTV. Rodolfo is an active member of Shanghai's maker and entrepreneur community.Rodolfo’s current research is centred on the intersection of accessibility, Human-Computer Interfaces, and maker tools. He closely collaborates with NGOs and companies in co-designing assistive technologies for people with disabilities. Rodolfo holds a Master of Fine Arts from the University of Plymouth, UK, and a Master of Science in Electronics Engineering from IESE, Argentina.
